message:
feat(entry-points): add Brenner Loop entry points from main site

- Add Research Lab CTA section to landing page (always visible)
  - Shows 4 operator preview cards
  - Links to sessions, operators, and tutorial
- Add "Try in Session" button to operator detail sheet
  - Links to /sessions/new with operator pre-selected

Implements brenner_bot-mat6 acceptance criteria:
- Landing page has clear CTA for sessions ✓
- Operator pages link to sessions ✓

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -573,7 +573,7 @@ {"id":"brenner_bot-ruzw","title":"Allow :::delta fenced blocks in delta parsing/validation","description":"README examples use :::delta, but parser and API validation only accepted ```delta. Accept :::delta as an alternative fence to prevent false validation errors.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-02T09:20:26.879836697-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T09:20:47.288672825-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T09:20:47.288672825-05:00","close_reason":"Delta parser + API validation now accept :::delta fences; added parser test."} {"id":"brenner_bot-s2vm","title":"Bug: compiler robustness for CRLF deltas + contributor timestamps","description":"# Problem 1: CRLF fenced delta blocks aren’t detected `apps/web/src/lib/delta-parser.ts` currently expects a newline after the ` ```delta` fence that is `\n` only. If a message uses Windows line endings (`\r\n`), deltas can fail to be extracted and compilation silently drops updates. # Problem 2: contributor timestamps not filled `apps/web/src/lib/artifact-merge.ts` `mergeArtifactWithTimestamps` only updates `contributed_at` when it’s already set. If an older artifact or an upstream tool produced a contributor entry without `contributed_at`, the merge doesn’t backfill it. # Fix - Make delta fence detection tolerate `\r?\n`. - In `mergeArtifactWithTimestamps`, backfill missing `contributed_at` deterministically. - Add/extend unit tests to prevent regressions. ","acceptance_criteria":"CRLF fenced delta blocks are extracted; mergeArtifactWithTimestamps fills missing contributed_at deterministically; targeted unit tests cover both and pass under bun.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenMountain","created_at":"2025-12-30T11:27:21.668055566-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T11:40:22.868523646-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T11:40:22.868523646-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: delta parser supports CRLF and mergeArtifactWithTimestamps backfills contributor timestamps.","labels":["compiler","quality"]} {"id":"brenner_bot-s4f","title":"TanStack Virtual: Virtualize QuoteBankViewer Grid","description":"# Goal Virtualize the QuoteBankViewer grid for smooth scrolling through large filtered result sets. ## Background The quote bank contains 100+ quotes. When filtered/searched: - All matching quotes render immediately - Large result sets cause scroll jank - Initial render can be slow ## Current Implementation ```typescript // QuoteBankViewer.tsx {filteredQuotes.map((quote, index) => ( ))} ``` ## Virtualization Approach ### Grid Virtualization Quotes display in a responsive grid: - Desktop: 2-3 columns - Mobile: 1 column TanStack Virtual supports grids via `useVirtualizer` with row-based virtualization. ### Implementation Strategy ```typescript function VirtualizedQuoteGrid({ quotes }) { const parentRef = useRef(null); const [columns, setColumns] = useState(2); // Update columns based on container width useEffect(() => { const observer = new ResizeObserver(entries => { const width = entries[0].contentRect.width; setColumns(width >= 768 ? 3 : width >= 640 ? 2 : 1); }); observer.observe(parentRef.current); return () => observer.disconnect(); }, []); const rowCount = Math.ceil(quotes.length / columns); const virtualizer = useVirtualizer({ count: rowCount, getScrollElement: () => parentRef.current, estimateSize: () => 200, // Estimated row height overscan: 2, }); return (
{virtualizer.getVirtualItems().map(virtualRow => { const startIndex = virtualRow.index * columns; const rowQuotes = quotes.slice(startIndex, startIndex + columns); return (
{rowQuotes.map(quote => ( ))}
); })}
); } ``` ## Considerations ### Responsive Columns Column count changes on resize. When this happens: - Row count changes - Virtual list must recalculate - Current scroll position should be preserved ### Card Height Variation Quote cards have variable height (quote length varies). Options: 1. Fixed height cards with text truncation (simpler) 2. Dynamic measurement (more complex but better UX) Recommendation: Start with fixed height, iterate if needed. ### Filter Changes When filter/search changes, result set changes entirely. - Reset scroll to top - Recalculate virtual list ### Empty State When no quotes match filter, show "No results" message. ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Quote grid virtualized - [ ] Responsive columns work - [ ] Filter changes handled smoothly - [ ] Scroll is smooth at 60fps - [ ] Empty state works - [ ] Tag filtering still works ## Files to Modify - apps/web/src/components/quotebank/QuoteBankViewer.tsx - apps/web/src/components/quotebank/VirtualizedQuoteGrid.tsx (create) - apps/web/src/components/quotebank/QuoteCard.tsx (ensure fixed height)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:13.679947704-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:43:57.170223089-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:43:57.170223089-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented TanStack Virtual for QuoteBankViewer: added useVirtualizer with dynamic height measurement, overscan, and proper scroll container. Build passes, all 532 tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-s4f","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1uu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T23:18:45.876624114-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} -{"id":"brenner_bot-s797","title":"Tutorial Path: Quick Start (7 Steps)","description":"# Purpose Implement the Quick Start tutorial path — 7 steps that take a user from "What is this?" to "I have a research artifact for my own question" in approximately 30 minutes. This is the **primary onboarding funnel**. Most users will take this path. It must be polished, failure-tolerant, and genuinely useful. --- # Core Constraint **No complex infrastructure required.** This path works with: - A terminal - Git - Bun - Any AI chat interface (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini — even free tiers) No Agent Mail. No ntm. No subscriptions beyond basic access. --- # Step-by-Step Breakdown ## Step 1: What Is This? (~3 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/1/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Explain BrennerBot in 60 seconds - Present the Two Axioms prominently - Show a completed artifact (screenshot or inline example) - Set expectations for the path **Key Content**: \`\`\`markdown # What Is BrennerBot? Sydney Brenner (1927-2019) was one of the most successful experimental biologists in history. But his **method** is more valuable than any single discovery. BrennerBot operationalizes that method for AI-assisted research. ## The Two Axioms 1. **Reality has a generative grammar** — the world is produced by causal machinery with discoverable rules. 2. **To understand is to reconstruct** — you haven't explained something until you can build it from primitives. ## What You'll Produce By the end of this tutorial, you'll have a **research artifact** for your own scientific question containing: - 2-5 competing hypotheses (including a "third alternative") - Discriminative tests that separate the hypotheses - An explicit assumption ledger - Adversarial critiques of your framing Let's start. \`\`\` --- ## Step 2: Prerequisites (~2 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/2/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - OS-specific instructions (tabs for Mac, Windows, Linux) - Interactive checklist (user confirms each prereq) - Skip affordance for experienced users **Prerequisites**: 1. Terminal access 2. Git installed 3. Bun installed **Code Blocks**: \`\`\`bash # Check git git --version # Check bun (or install) bun --version || curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash # Verify bun --version \`\`\` **Troubleshooting**: - "bun: command not found" → Add to PATH, restart terminal - Windows: Use WSL2 (link to instructions) - Permission errors: sudo or user install --- ## Step 3: Clone & Install (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/3/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Get the repo locally - Install dependencies - Verify installation with doctor command **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Clone the repository git clone https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/brenner_bot.git cd brenner_bot # Install dependencies bun install # Verify everything works ./brenner doctor \`\`\` **Expected Output**: \`\`\` BrennerBot Doctor Report ======================== ✓ Bun runtime: 1.x.x ✓ Dependencies installed ✓ Corpus files present ✓ CLI executable ⚠ Agent Mail: not running (optional for Quick Start) \`\`\` **Checkpoint**: "You've cloned BrennerBot and verified your installation!" --- ## Step 4: Search the Corpus (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/4/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Teach corpus search - Show how results include §n anchors - User searches for their own topic **Interactive Example**: \`\`\`bash # Search for a topic ./brenner corpus search "pattern formation" # Try your own domain ./brenner corpus search "YOUR_TOPIC_HERE" \`\`\` **Explanation**: - Results show transcript segments with §n references - These are stable anchors for citation - High-quality matches ground your research in Brenner's thinking **User Exercise**: "Search for something related to YOUR research domain. Note 3-5 §n references that seem relevant." --- ## Step 5: Build an Excerpt (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/5/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Compose relevant transcript sections into an excerpt - Explain why excerpts matter (grounding, citation) - Save excerpt for use in next step **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Build excerpt from specific sections ./brenner excerpt build --sections 58,78,161 > my_excerpt.md # Or use search results to identify sections ./brenner corpus search "your topic" --format sections ./brenner excerpt build --sections THOSE_SECTIONS > my_excerpt.md \`\`\` **Excerpt Template**: \`\`\`markdown # Research Excerpt ## Relevant Brenner Segments ### §58: Reduction to one dimension "The best thing in science is to work out of phase..." ### §78: The importance of choice "You have to choose the right problem..." [Your selected segments here] \`\`\` --- ## Step 6: Your First Session (~8 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/6/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Compose a kickoff prompt using CLI - Run in "local mode" (no Agent Mail) - User copies output to their AI chat interface **The Big Moment**: This is where users actually DO Brenner-style research. **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Compose a kickoff prompt (local mode) ./brenner prompt compose \\ --excerpt-file my_excerpt.md \\ --question "How do cells determine their position in a developing embryo?" \\ --role unified \\ --output-file my_prompt.md # View the composed prompt cat my_prompt.md \`\`\` **User Action**: 1. Copy the contents of my_prompt.md 2. Paste into Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini 3. Run the conversation 4. Save the AI's response **Pro Tip**: "The prompt includes specific instructions for the AI to produce structured output. Don't modify these instructions — they encode the Brenner methodology." --- ## Step 7: Understand the Output (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/7/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Walk through each artifact section - Explain why each matters - Connect back to Brenner's method **Artifact Sections Explained**: **Hypothesis Slate**: - 2-5 competing explanations - Must include "third alternative" (both leading theories could be wrong) - Each has mechanism + anchors **Discriminative Tests**: - Tests that SEPARATE hypotheses, not just confirm one - Potency checks ensure negative results mean something - Object transposition: choose the system that makes the test cheap **Assumption Ledger**: - Explicit load-bearing beliefs - Scale/physics constraints - Everything the hypotheses depend on **Adversarial Critique**: - Attacks on the framing itself - "Real third alternative" check - Forces you to think harder **Checkpoint**: "You've applied the Brenner method to your own research question!" **Next Steps**: - Refine your artifact based on the critique section - Try the Agent-Assisted path for higher leverage - Explore the Multi-Agent Cockpit for serious research programs --- # Testing Requirements - E2E: complete path from step 1 to step 7 - Test on Mac, Windows (WSL), Linux - Test with slow network (large clone) - Test with pre-existing brenner_bot clone (idempotent) --- # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 7 steps implemented with full content - [ ] Code blocks have copy functionality - [ ] Troubleshooting section for each step - [ ] Checkpoints at steps 3 and 7 - [ ] Mobile-responsive design - [ ] Time estimates accurate (tested) - [ ] Works with only free AI tiers","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:50:48.557736851-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:12:10.607207668-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-s797","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.466878203-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} +{"id":"brenner_bot-s797","title":"Tutorial Path: Quick Start (7 Steps)","description":"# Purpose Implement the Quick Start tutorial path — 7 steps that take a user from "What is this?" to "I have a research artifact for my own question" in approximately 30 minutes. This is the **primary onboarding funnel**. Most users will take this path. It must be polished, failure-tolerant, and genuinely useful. --- # Core Constraint **No complex infrastructure required.** This path works with: - A terminal - Git - Bun - Any AI chat interface (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini — even free tiers) No Agent Mail. No ntm. No subscriptions beyond basic access. --- # Step-by-Step Breakdown ## Step 1: What Is This? Note 3-5 §n references that seem relevant." --- ## Step 5: Build an Excerpt (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/5/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Compose relevant transcript sections into an excerpt - Explain why excerpts matter (grounding, citation) - Save excerpt for use in next step **Commands**: \`\`\`bash # Build excerpt from specific sections ./brenner excerpt build --sections 58,78,161 > my_excerpt.md # Or use search results to identify sections ./brenner corpus search "your topic" --format sections ./brenner excerpt build --sections THOSE_SECTIONS > my_excerpt.md \`\`\` **Excerpt Template**: \`\`\`markdown # Research Excerpt ## Relevant Brenner Segments ### §58: Reduction to one dimension "The best thing in science is to work out of phase..." ### §78: The importance of choice "You have to choose the right problem..." Copy the contents of my_prompt.md 2. Paste into Claude, ChatGPT, or Gemini 3. Run the conversation 4. Save the AI's response **Pro Tip**: "The prompt includes specific instructions for the AI to produce structured output. Don't modify these instructions — they encode the Brenner methodology." --- ## Step 7: Understand the Output (~5 min) **File**: app/tutorial/quick-start/7/page.tsx **Content Goals**: - Walk through each artifact section - Explain why each matters - Connect back to Brenner's method **Artifact Sections Explained**: **Hypothesis Slate**: - 2-5 competing explanations - Must include "third alternative" (both leading theories could be wrong) - Each has mechanism + anchors **Discriminative Tests**: - Tests that SEPARATE hypotheses, not just confirm one - Potency checks ensure negative results mean something - Object transposition: choose the system that makes the test cheap **Assumption Ledger**: - Explicit load-bearing beliefs - Scale/physics constraints - Everything the hypotheses depend on **Adversarial Critique**: - Attacks on the framing itself - "Real third alternative" check - Forces you to think harder **Checkpoint**: "You've applied the Brenner method to your own research question!" **Next Steps**: - Test on Mac, Windows (WSL), Linux - Test with slow network (large clone) - Test with pre-existing brenner_bot clone (idempotent) --- # Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All 7 steps implemented with full content - [ ] Code blocks have copy functionality - [ ] Troubleshooting section for each step - [ ] Checkpoints at steps 3 and 7 - [ ] Mobile-responsive design - [ ] Time estimates accurate (tested) - [ ] Works with only free AI tiers","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:50:48.557736851-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:14:56.053192238-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-s797","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-do3j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T14:54:04.466878203-05:00","created_by":"daemon","metadata":"{}"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-s8x","title":"Optimize mobile transcript performance with progressive loading","description":"## Context Mobile scroll was fixed (commit 8036000) by rendering all sections in document flow instead of using virtualization. This works but has performance implications. ## Current State - Lighthouse mobile score: 80-81 - FCP: 2.7s (target: <1.5s) - LCP: 4.2s (target: <2.5s) - Script evaluation: ~7.3 seconds for 234 sections ## Implementation (BLOCKED) The implementation was completed multiple times but keeps getting reverted by an external file watcher or linter process. The code changes are: ### 1. Add constants and state (lines 571-584) \`\`\`typescript const MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS = 8; const MOBILE_LOAD_INCREMENT = 8; // In component: const [mobileSectionsLoaded, setMobileSectionsLoaded] = useState(MOBILE_INITIAL_SECTIONS); const loadMoreSentinelRef = useRef(null); \`\`\` ### 2. Add IntersectionObserver effect (after virtualizer) \`\`\`typescript useEffect(() => { if (typeof window === "undefined" || window.innerWidth >= 1024) return; const sentinel = loadMoreSentinelRef.current; if (!sentinel) return; const observer = new IntersectionObserver( (entries) => { if (entries[0]?.isIntersecting && mobileSectionsLoaded < data.sections.length) { setMobileSectionsLoaded((n) => Math.min(n + MOBILE_LOAD_INCREMENT, data.sections.length)); } }, { rootMargin: "300px" } ); observer.observe(sentinel); return () => observer.disconnect(); }, [mobileSectionsLoaded, data.sections.length]); \`\`\` ### 3. Update mobile rendering \`\`\`typescript {data.sections.slice(0, mobileSectionsLoaded).map(...)} {mobileSectionsLoaded < data.sections.length && (
Loading spinner...
)} \`\`\` ### 4. Update scrollToSection Handle loading target section before scrolling. ## BLOCKER File changes to TranscriptViewer.tsx are automatically reverted by some process. Need to identify and disable the watcher/hook before this can be implemented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-30T02:24:48.653428197-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T02:32:57.850333349-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T02:29:59.563357022-05:00","close_reason":"Progressive loading implemented: Initial 8 sections load on mobile, IntersectionObserver with 300px rootMargin loads 8 more as user scrolls, scrollToSection loads target section before scrolling. Build passes."} {"id":"brenner_bot-se2r","title":"TASK: Define Hypothesis Lifecycle State Machine","description":" ## Formal States for Hypothesis Evolution Hypotheses aren't just "open" or "closed" - they have a rich lifecycle. This task defines the formal state machine. ### Hypothesis States 1. **Draft** - Initial creation, freely editable - No predictions locked yet - Can be deleted without penalty 2. **Active** - User is actively working on this hypothesis - Has at least one locked prediction - Cannot be deleted (only superseded or falsified) 3. **Testing** - Evidence collection in progress - Predictions locked, awaiting results - Confidence updates happening 4. **Supported** - Evidence consistent with hypothesis - High robustness score (passed tests) - May still be tested further 5. **Falsified** - Clear discriminative evidence against - Moved to Hypothesis Graveyard - Learning captured (what did we learn?) 6. **Superseded** - Replaced by a better formulation - Link to successor hypothesis - Still visible in history 7. **Dormant** - User paused work (no activity for N days) - Can be reactivated - Preserved but not shown in active list ### State Transitions - Draft → Active: First prediction locked - Active → Testing: Evidence collection starts - Testing → Supported: Predictions confirmed - Testing → Falsified: Predictions refuted - Active → Superseded: User creates refined version - Active → Dormant: No activity for 30 days - Dormant → Active: User resumes work ### Implementation - Add `state` field to HypothesisCard - State transition functions with validation - UI indicators for each state (icons, colors) - Filters in session list by state ### Depends On - an1n.1 (HypothesisCard Interface) ","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:23.309616577-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T11:43:52.589020594-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T11:43:52.589020594-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented hypothesis lifecycle state machine with 7 states (draft, active, testing, supported, falsified, superseded, dormant), transition guards, UI configuration, and lifecycle statistics","labels":["brenner-loop","hypothesis-engine","state-machine"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-se2r","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-an1n.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:51:47.763351355-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"brenner_bot-sedg","title":"FEATURE: Undo/Redo System for Session Actions","description":" ## Research is Iterative Users will make mistakes and want to undo: - Accidentally locked a prediction too early - Recorded wrong evidence observation - Updated confidence incorrectly - Discarded a hypothesis prematurely ### Command Pattern Implementation ```typescript interface SessionCommand { id: string; type: string; timestamp: Date; // For undo execute(): Promise; undo(): Promise; // For display description: string; } class UpdateConfidenceCommand implements SessionCommand { constructor( private hypothesisId: string, private previousConfidence: number, private newConfidence: number, private evidenceId: string ) {} async execute() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.newConfidence); } async undo() { await updateHypothesisConfidence(this.hypothesisId, this.previousConfidence); await markEvidenceReverted(this.evidenceId); } get description() { return `Update confidence: ${this.previousConfidence}% → ${this.newConfidence}%`; } } ``` ### Undo Stack Management ```typescript interface UndoManager { history: SessionCommand[]; undoneStack: SessionCommand[]; // For redo maxHistory: number; execute(command: SessionCommand): Promise; undo(): Promise; redo(): Promise; canUndo: boolean; canRedo: boolean; } ``` ### Undoable Actions - Confidence updates - Hypothesis state changes - Evidence recording - Prediction modifications (before lock) - Session phase transitions (with warning) ### Non-Undoable Actions (by design) - Prediction LOCK (intentionally irreversible) - Session export/share - Agent dispatch (can't un-ask an agent) ### UI Integration ``` ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ Session: Social Media Hypothesis │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ [↩ Undo] [↪ Redo] History ▾ │ ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤ │ │ │ Recent Actions: │ │ • Updated confidence 72% → 49% [↩] │ │ • Recorded evidence: Algorithm... [↩] │ │ • Applied Level Split operator [↩] │ │ │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘ ``` ### Keyboard Shortcuts - Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z: Undo - Ctrl+Shift+Z / Cmd+Shift+Z: Redo ### Persistence Undo history persisted with session (LocalStorage): - Survives page refresh - Cleared on session close ### File Location `apps/web/src/lib/brenner-loop/undoManager.ts` `apps/web/src/hooks/useUndoRedo.ts` `apps/web/src/components/brenner-loop/UndoRedoControls.tsx` ### Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Core undo/redo works for main actions - [ ] History preserved across refresh - [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work - [ ] Non-undoable actions clearly marked ### Depends On - 1v26.3 (Session Context Provider) - reew.3 (Session State Machine) ","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:01.727561365-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:01.727561365-05:00","labels":["brenner-loop","session-ui","undo-redo"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-sedg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-1v26.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:42.539185749-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"brenner_bot-sedg","depends_on_id":"brenner_bot-reew.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T03:18:44.070222339-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -165,6 +165,73 @@ export default function Home() { + {/* Research Lab CTA - Always visible */} +
+
+ {/* Decorative elements */} +
+
+ +
+ {/* Header */} +
+
+ + Research Lab +
+

+ Apply the Method to Your Research +

+

+ The Brenner Loop is an interactive research framework that helps you develop discriminative hypotheses using four cognitive operators. Run structured sessions and track your evolving understanding. +
